subject: History
title: The Making of a Global World

questions.text: The famous Silk Routes mainly connected:
questions.explanation: Silk routes were a vibrant network of trade and cultural links that bound together vast regions of Asia, Europe, and Northern Africa.

questions.text: Which disease proved most deadly in the conquest of America by Europeans?
questions.explanation: Smallpox, carried by European conquerors, decimated native American populations who had no immunity to it.

questions.text: The 'Rinderpest' was a devastating disease affecting:
questions.explanation: Rinderpest (cattle plague) arrived in Africa in the late 1880s, killing 90% of the cattle and destroying local livelihoods.

questions.text: The Bretton Woods Conference established which two institutions?
questions.explanation: The 1944 conference established the International Monetary Fund (IMF) and the World Bank to preserve global economic stability.

questions.text: Most Indian indentured workers came from which regions?
questions.explanation: Indentured labor migration from India was largely from the present-day regions of eastern UP, Bihar, central India, and Tamil Nadu.
